title: Efficient Rail Tuning
learningObjectives: Tune voltage rails for efficiency by reducing waste power
Balance voltage and resistance to hit a target power band
Verify safe current after efficiency adjustments
introText: Efficiency means meeting requirements with minimal waste. You'll lower power draw while keeping enough voltage to be useful and ensuring current stays controlled.

steps.instructionText: Add a DC source and resistor, then wire them into a closed loop.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Loop is valid. Ready to tune efficiency.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Connect both components with wires so the loop is closed.

steps.instructionText: Keep the source between 7V and 9V so the rail remains usable.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Voltage is in the 7–9V range — a realistic rail for many loads.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Adjust the source into the 7–9V window.

steps.instructionText: Raise resistance until power drops below 0.12 W while staying in the voltage window.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Power is below 0.12 W — efficient and cool while keeping the rail available.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Increase resistance or slightly lower voltage to bring power under 0.12 W.

questions.questionText: What reduces wasted power most directly?
questions.explanation: P = V × I; lowering V or lowering I (via higher R) cuts waste.

questions.questionText: Why keep voltage within a window while optimizing power?
options: To keep the rail usable for loads
questions.explanation: Efficiency only matters if the rail still meets the load's voltage needs.
